text: The India AI impact Summit 2026 opened with the world watching. It did not open smoothly though. But within hours, the headlines no longer were about the trillion dollar AI dream. Instead, were about chaos, controversy, and about a robotic dog whose bite was stronger than its bark. I'm of course going to pull back the curtains on what really happened. Day one at Bharat Mandapam started more like a gridlock. Long cues, overcrowding, delegates trapped outside for hours. But when we stepped inside was not so very different as it gave way to pure scramble and chaos, security sweeps for VIPs, booths shut down. Some founders actually lost access to their own very stalls. And the friction caused by all of this was very unsettling, forcing the IT minister himself to step in and apologize. Then something shifted online. At a university stall, Galgotias, a robotic dog named Orion was presented as a fully in-house creation. There were smiles for the cameras. Then the viewers began making comparisons because Orion appeared to belong to a different breed, one already on sale in China. And guess the Sherlocks on the internet made it look elementary. The final finding Orion wasn't Indian. It was the Unitary GO2. A Chinese-made bot you can buy for 2 to three lak rupees. busted, exposed, and caught in 4K. The authorities kicked them out. Obviously, the university tried to backpedal, claimed it was just a learning tool, but the video receipts were already out. So, suddenly the whole summit was asking, "Are we building or just buying?" Bengaluru's startup Neo Sapion had their AI variables vanish during a VIP security sweep only to recover them later. The panic turned into an accidental masterclass in viral marketing that absolutely broke the internet. And of course, how can we not address the elephant in the room while summarizing the summit till now, which is the hardware? When one walks the expo floor, you cannot help but notice that much of what you see have traces back to Chinese manufacturing, which is not unusual. Modern supply chains are global and components cross borders long before logos are applied. But you know what? It does kind of complicate the story which is being told. We all know India wants sovereign AI. But where does sovereignity actually begin if the very synthetic ground we are walking on comes from global supply chains? That's an uncomfortable question. So the point is are we innovating or are we just integrating? But of course we should not just focus on the cynical voices. Real work is also very much a part of the conversation because only a few hours back unveiled 30B and 105B parameter foundation models positioning itself against global leaders such as Chat GPT, Gemini, Claude and Deep Seek. The 30B model hits a very crucial efficiency sweet spot, balancing performance and compute, making it ideal for conversational AI translation and enterprise deployment. Built and

few hours back unveiled 30B and 105B parameter foundation models positioning itself against global leaders such as Chat GPT, Gemini, Claude and Deep Seek. The 30B model hits a very crucial efficiency sweet spot, balancing performance and compute, making it ideal for conversational AI translation and enterprise deployment. Built and languages, enabling AI access at national scale. Sarbam also demonstrated Vikram, its chatbot that can function even on a feature phone. The 105B model that I mentioned earlier targets complex reasoning and high intelligence tasks showcasing capabilities like financial analysis and document comprehensions that actually traditionally take hours now are delivered in seconds. Backed by over $53 million in funding, Serbam is building sovereign AI infrastructure designed for India's linguistic diversity, enterprise needs and of course the realworld deployment. Healthcare AI announcing diagnostic costs for rural India. A very poant moment. AI imaging hunting down TB, diabetic retinoph retinopathy and cancer. defining real impact. Firefighting robots sniffing out danger zones with AI sensors. Autonomous tractors taking over the intensive manual farm jobs. These account for as the homegrown wins which we must go ahead and congratulate. Three days done, three more to go for the summit of course because the summit has been extended by one more day and the big guns are still loading. Serveria is gearing up to announce India's first fully indigenous LLM. Bahen is pushing multilingual AI infrastructure which is compute expansion sovereign models which means the money is flowing irrespective of everyone still buzzing about the robotic dog controversy. Here is as always the front page take. India wants the worldclass crown and why not? But the execution unfortunately has been tacky and lacking. When you invite global leaders and celebrate thousands of GPUs, you do not serve them long cues and an underwhelming stage setup. You actually want to go ahead and figure out because optics do matter and so does the experience you serve up that stays with the people for long. India has the ambition now it needs the polish. Sloppy beginnings accompany revolutions but you know what they shouldn't define them. Moand Aspire yesterday on front page had said the summit and all should happen in Delhi man but the real gravity is in Bengaluru which has rightly won the title of being the real tech theater. Deep mind CEO Demi Hazabis knows it too. He was just at IISC Bengaluru scoping out the research depth. Pi estimates we need 50,000 K rupees in risk capital to keep builders right here to scale innovation to compete in the big leagues. India's AI moment will not be defined by flashy events. That's correct. It will be defined by cold hard capital and more importantly relentless execution by having the right people at the right positions and a ruthless ecosystem. A IM has biggest and oldest AI summit cipher 2026 which is of course starting from the October 7th to 9th 2026 at KDPO Whitefield Bengaluru as we always say this this time we are expecting more people but our well estimate is more than 5,000 plus attendees expected each day 100 plus speakers live demonstrations and interactive workshops. Cyber 202, Cipher 2026 is where India's AI ecosystem meets the future. And of course, Wi-Fi is going to be front and center. Do not miss this one. This, ladies and gentlemen, is front page by the AIM network. Like, share, subscribe, and always remember, think AI. Think AIM, man.

title: India AI Summit Highlights Innovation Also Sparks Authenticity Debate
